53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)
371	            if self_val is not None and other_val is not None:
372	                setattr(self, item, torch.cat([self_val, other_val]))
373	
374	        self.is_all_greedy &= other.is_all_greedy
375	        self.need_top_p_sampling |= other.need_top_p_sampling
376	        self.need_top_k_sampling |= other.need_top_k_sampling
377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling
378	
379	        self.adjusted_merge_batch(other)
380	
381	    def copy_for_forward(self):
382	        # Accumulate the penalty into a pre-allocated buffer to get rid of the dependency of `penalizer_orchestrator` later
383	        self.update_penalties()
384	        return dataclasses.replace(self, penalizer_orchestrator=None)
385	
386	
387	def merge_bias_tensor(
388	    lhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
389	    rhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
390	    bs1: int,
391	    bs2: int,
392	    device: str,
393	    default: float,
394	):
395	    """Merge two bias tensors for batch merging.
396	
397	    Args:
398	        lhs: Left-hand side tensor
399	        rhs: Right-hand side tensor
400	        bs1: Batch size of left-hand side tensor
401	        bs2: Batch size of right-hand side tensor
402	        device: Device to place the merged tensor on
403	        default: Default value for missing tensor elements
404	
405	    Returns:
406	        Merged tensor or None if both inputs are None
407	    """
408	    if lhs is None and rhs is None:
409	        return None
410	
411	    if lhs is not None and rhs is not None:
412	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
413	    else:
414	        if lhs is not None:
415	            shape, dtype = lhs.shape[1:], lhs.dtype
416	        else:
417	            shape, dtype = rhs.shape[1:], rhs.dtype
418	
419	        if lhs is None:
420	            lhs = torch.empty((bs1, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
421	        if rhs is None:
422	            rhs = torch.empty((bs2, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
423	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
424

16	import json
17	import logging
18	import os
19	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
20	
21	import torch
22	from llguidance import LLMatcher, LLTokenizer, StructTag, grammar_from
23	from llguidance.hf import from_tokenizer
24	from llguidance.torch import (
25	    allocate_token_bitmask,
26	    apply_token_bitmask_inplace,
27	    fill_next_token_bitmask,
28	)
29	
30	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
31	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
32	    BaseGrammarBackend,
33	    BaseGrammarObject,
34	)
35	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag

37	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

39	
40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92	
93	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None
99	
100	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
101	        return "", -1
102	
103	    def jump_and_retokenize(
104	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
105	    ):
106	        pass

108	
109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
179

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state
91

94	    program,
95	    backend,
96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for reasoner grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
17	
18	import torch
19	
20	from .base_grammar_backend import (
21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
22	    BaseGrammarBackend,
23	    BaseGrammarObject,
24	)
25	
26	
27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )
107	
108	
109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj

316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"

1	"""Tracing a program."""
2	
3	import uuid
4	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
5	
6	from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
7	from sglang.lang.interpreter import ProgramState, ProgramStateGroup
8	from sglang.lang.ir import (
9	    SglArgument,
10	    SglConstantText,
11	    SglExpr,
12	    SglExprList,
13	    SglFork,
14	    SglGen,
15	    SglGetForkItem,
16	    SglRoleBegin,
17	    SglRoleEnd,
18	    SglSelect,
19	    SglVariable,
20	    SglVarScopeBegin,
21	    SglVarScopeEnd,
22	)
23	
24	
25	class StopTracing(Exception):
26	    pass
27	
28	
29	def extract_prefix_by_tracing(program, backend):
30	    # Create dummy arguments
31	    dummy_arguments = {name: SglArgument(name, None) for name in program.arg_names}
32	    arguments = dummy_arguments
33	    arguments.update(program.bind_arguments)
34	
35	    # Trace
36	    tracer = TracerProgramState(backend, arguments, only_trace_prefix=True)
37	    try:
38	        with TracingScope(tracer):
39	            tracer.ret_value = program.func(tracer, **arguments)
40	    except (StopTracing, TypeError, AttributeError):
41	        # Some exceptions may not be caught
42	        pass
43	
44	    # Run and cache prefix
45	    prefix = ""
46	    for expr in tracer.flatten_nodes():
47	        if isinstance(expr, SglConstantText):
48	            prefix += expr.value
49	        else:
50	            break
51	    return prefix
52	
53	
54	def trace_program(program, arguments, backend):
55	    # Create dummy backend
56	    if backend is None:
57	        backend = BaseBackend()
58	
59	    # Create dummy arguments
60	    dummy_arguments = {
61	        name: SglArgument(name, None)
62	        for name in program.arg_names
63	        if name not in arguments
64	    }
65	    arguments.update(dummy_arguments)
66	    arguments.update(program.bind_arguments)
67	
68	    # Trace
69	    tracer = TracerProgramState(backend, arguments, only_trace_prefix=False)
70	    with TracingScope(tracer):
71	        tracer.ret_value = program.func(tracer, **arguments)
72	    return tracer
73	
74	
75	class TracerProgramState(ProgramState):
76	    def __init__(self, backend, arguments, only_trace_prefix):
77	        self.pid = uuid.uuid4().hex
78	        self.backend = backend
79	        self.arguments: Dict[str, Any] = arguments
80	        self.only_trace_prefix = only_trace_prefix
81	
82	        if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
83	            self.backend = backend.endpoint
84	
85	        self.nodes = []
86	        self.last_node = None
87	        self.variables = {}
88	        self.ret_value = None
89	
90	        # For completion
91	
92	        # For chat
93	        self.messages_ = []
94	        self.cur_role = None
95	        self.chat_template = self.backend.get_chat_template()
96	
97	        # For multi states
98	        self.child_states = []
99	
100	        cur_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
101	        if cur_scope is not None:
102	            cur_scope.add_child_state(self)
103	
104	    ##################################
105	    ########### Public API ###########
106	    ##################################
107	
108	    def fork(self, size: int = 1, position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None):
109	        assert size >= 1
110	
111	        if self.only_trace_prefix:
112	            raise StopTracing()
113	
114	        fork_node = SglFork(size)
115	        fork_node.prev_node = self.last_node
116	
117	        states = [
118	            TracerProgramState(self.backend, self.arguments, self.only_trace_prefix)
119	            for _ in range(size)
120	        ]
121	
122	        for i in range(size):
123	            node = SglGetForkItem(i)
124	            node.prev_node = fork_node
125	            states[i].last_node = node
126	            states[i].variables = dict(self.variables)
127	            states[i].messages_ = list(self.messages_)
128	            states[i].cur_role = self.cur_role
129	            states[i].chat_template = self.chat_template
130	
131	        state_group = ProgramStateGroup(states, self)
132	
133	        return state_group
134	
135	    ##################################
136	    ########## Internal API ##########
137	    ##################################
138	
139	    def _append_node(self, other: SglExpr):
140	        self.nodes.append(other)
141	        other.prev_node = self.last_node
142	        self.last_node = other
143	
144	    def _execute(self, other: SglExpr):
145	        if isinstance(other, str):
146	            other = SglConstantText(other)
147	
148	        other.pid = self.pid
149	
150	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
151	            self._execute_fill(other)
152	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
153	            self._execute_gen(other)
154	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
155	            self._execute_select(other)
156	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
157	            for x in other.expr_list:
158	                self._execute(x)
159	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
160	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
161	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
162	            self._execute_role_end(other)
163	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
164	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
165	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
166	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
167	        else:
168	            if self.only_trace_prefix:
169	                raise StopTracing()
170	            else:
171	                self._append_node(other)
172	
173	        return self
174	
175	    def __iadd__(self, other):
176	        self._execute(other)
177	        return self
178	
179	    def _execute_fill(self, expr: SglConstantText):
180	        if isinstance(expr, str):
181	            expr = SglConstantText(expr)
182	        self._append_node(expr)
183	
184	    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
185	        name = expr.name if expr.name is not None else "gen_" + str(len(self.variables))
186	        new_node = SglVariable(name, source=expr)
187	        self.variables[name] = new_node
188	        self._append_node(expr)
189	
190	    def _execute_select(self, expr: SglSelect):
191	        name = (
192	            expr.name if expr.name is not None else "select_" + str(len(self.variables))
193	        )
194	        new_node = SglVariable(name, source=expr)
195	        self.variables[name] = new_node
196	        self._append_node(expr)
197	
198	    def _execute_role_begin(self, expr: SglRoleBegin):
199	        assert self.cur_role is None, "Nested roles are not allowed."
200	
201	        if len(self.messages_) == 0 and expr.role != "system":
202	            # Insert default system message
203	            default_system = self.chat_template.default_system_prompt
204	            if default_system:
205	                self._execute_role_begin(SglRoleBegin("system"))
206	                self._execute_fill(default_system)
207	                self._execute_role_end(SglRoleEnd("system"))
208	
209	        self.cur_role = expr.role
210	
211	        prefix, suffix = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(
212	            expr.role, self.messages_
213	        )
214	
215	        self._execute_fill(prefix)
216	
217	    def _execute_role_end(self, expr: SglRoleEnd):
218	        prefix, suffix = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(
219	            expr.role, self.messages_
220	        )
221	
222	        self._execute_fill(suffix)
223	
224	        self.messages_.append({"role": expr.role, "content": ""})
225	
226	        self.cur_role = None
227	
228	    def _execute_var_scope_end(self, expr: SglVarScopeEnd):
229	        new_node = SglVariable(expr.name, source=self.last_node)
230	        self.variables[expr.name] = new_node
231	
232	    def get_var(self, name):
233	        ret = self.arguments.get(name, None)
234	        if ret is not None:
235	            return ret
236	
237	        v = self.variables[name]
238	        return SglVariable(v.name, v.source)
239	
240	    def flatten_nodes(self):
241	        def traverse(cur):
242	            if isinstance(cur, SglExprList):
243	                for child in cur.expr_list:
244	                    traverse(child)
245	            else:
246	                ret.append(cur)
247	
248	        ret = []
249	        for x in self.nodes:
250	            traverse(x)
251	        return ret
252	
253	    def __del__(self):
254	        pass
255	
256	
257	class TracingScope:
258	    cur_scope = None
259	
260	    def __init__(self, tracer_state: TracerProgramState):
261	        self.tracer_state = tracer_state
262	        self.last_scope = TracingScope.cur_scope
263	
264	    def __enter__(self):
265	        TracingScope.cur_scope = self
266	        return self
267	
268	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
269	        TracingScope.cur_scope = self.last_scope
270	
271	    @staticmethod
272	    def get_current_scope():
273	        return TracingScope.cur_scope
274	
275	    def add_child_state(self, state: TracerProgramState):
276	        cur_scope = self
277	        while cur_scope is not None:
278	            cur_scope.tracer_state.child_states.append(state)
279	            cur_scope = cur_scope.last_scope
280

144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

250	class StreamExecutor:
251	    """A stream executor that executes SGL expressions in a background thread."""
252	
253	    def __init__(
254	        self,
255	        backend,
256	        arguments,
257	        default_sampling_para,
258	        chat_template,
259	        stream,
260	        num_api_spec_tokens=None,
261	        use_thread=True,
262	    ):
263	        from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
264	
265	        self.sid = uuid.uuid4().hex
266	        self.backend: BaseBackend = backend
267	        self.arguments: Dict[str, Any] = arguments
268	        self.default_sampling_para = default_sampling_para
269	        self.stream = stream
270	
271	        self.variables = {}  # Dict[name: str -> value: str]
272	        self.variable_event = {}  # Dict[name: str -> event: threading.Event]
273	        self.meta_info = {}  # Dict[name: str -> info: str]
274	        self.is_finished = False
275	        self.error_ = None
276	
277	        # For completion
278	        self.text_ = ""  # The full text
279	
280	        # For chat
281	        self.messages_ = []  # The messages in the OpenAI API format
282	        self.chat_template = chat_template or self.backend.get_chat_template()
283	        self.cur_role = None
284	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = None
285	
286	        # For vision
287	        self.images_ = []
288	        self.cur_images = []
289	
290	        # For fork/join
291	        self.fork_start_text_pos = None
292	
293	        # For speculative execution
294	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
295	        self.speculated_text = ""
296	
297	        # Worker thread
298	        self.use_thread = use_thread
299	        if self.use_thread:
300	            self.queue = queue.Queue()
301	
302	            def _run_worker_in_context():
303	                self._thread_worker_func()
304	
305	            self.worker = threading.Thread(
306	                target=contextvars.copy_context().run, args=(_run_worker_in_context,)
307	            )
308	            self.worker.start()
309	
310	        # For streaming
311	        if stream:
312	            self.stream_text_event = threading.Event()
313	            self.stream_var_event = {}
314	        else:
315	            self.stream_text_event = None
316	            self.stream_var_event = None
317	
318	    def submit(self, expr: SglExpr):
319	        self._init_var_event(expr)
320	
321	        if self.use_thread:
322	            self.queue.put(expr)
323	        else:
324	            self._execute(expr)
325	
326	    def sync(self):
327	        if self.use_thread:
328	            self.queue.join()
329	
330	    def get_var(self, name):
331	        if name in self.variable_event:
332	            self.variable_event[name].wait()
333	        return self.variables[name]
334	
335	    def set_var(self, name, value):
336	        self.variables[name] = value
337	
338	    def get_meta_info(self, name, timeout=None):
339	        if name in self.variable_event:
340	            got = self.variable_event[name].wait(timeout)
341	            if not got:
342	                raise TimeoutError(f"Timeout while waiting for event '{name}'")
343	        ret = self.meta_info.get(name, None)
344	        return ret
345	
346	    def fork(
347	        self,
348	        size: int = 1,
349	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
350	    ):
351	        if size > 1 and str(self.text_):
352	            self.submit(SglCommitLazy())
353	
354	        self.sync()
355	        size = int(size)
356	
357	        exes = [
358	            StreamExecutor(
359	                self.backend,
360	                self.arguments,
361	                self.default_sampling_para,
362	                self.chat_template,
363	                self.stream,
364	            )
365	            for _ in range(size)
366	        ]
367	        for i in range(size):
368	            exes[i].variables = dict(self.variables)
369	            exes[i].text_ = str(self.text_)
370	            exes[i].messages_ = list(self.messages_)
371	            exes[i].cur_role = self.cur_role
372	            exes[i].cur_role_begin_pos = self.cur_role_begin_pos
373	            exes[i].fork_start_text_pos = len(self.text_)
374	            exes[i].images_ = list(self.images_)
375	
376	            # TODO(ying): handle API speculative execution
377	
378	        return exes
379	
380	    def text(self):
381	        self.sync()
382	        return self.text_
383	
384	    def messages(self):
385	        self.sync()
386	        return self.messages_
387	
388	    def error(self):
389	        self.sync()
390	        return self.error_
391	
392	    def end(self):
393	        if self.use_thread:
394	            if self.worker.is_alive():
395	                self.queue.put(None)
396	        self.backend.end_program(self)
397	
398	    def _thread_worker_func(self):
399	        error = None
400	
401	        while True:
402	            expr = self.queue.get()
403	            if expr is None:
404	                self.queue.task_done()
405	                break
406	
407	            try:
408	                self._execute(expr)
409	            except Exception as e:
410	                warnings.warn(f"Error in stream_executor: {get_exception_traceback()}")
411	                error = e
412	                break
413	            self.queue.task_done()
414	            if self.stream_text_event:
415	                self.stream_text_event.set()
416	
417	        # Clean the queue and events
418	        if error is not None:
419	            try:
420	                while True:
421	                    self.queue.task_done()
422	                    self.queue.get_nowait()
423	            except queue.Empty:
424	                pass
425	            for name in self.variable_event:
426	                self.variable_event[name].set()
427	            if self.stream_var_event:
428	                for name in self.stream_var_event:
429	                    self.stream_var_event[name].set()
430	            self.error_ = error
431	
432	        if self.stream_text_event:
433	            self.stream_text_event.set()
434	
435	        self.is_finished = True
436	
437	    def _execute(self, other):
438	        if isinstance(other, str):
439	            other = SglConstantText(other)
440	
441	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
442	
443	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
444	            self._execute_fill(other.value)
445	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
446	            self._execute_gen(other)
447	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
448	            self._execute_select(other)
449	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
450	            for x in other.expr_list:
451	                self._execute(x)
452	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
453	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
454	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
455	            self._execute_role_end(other)
456	        elif isinstance(other, SglImage):
457	            self._execute_image(other)
458	        elif isinstance(other, SglVideo):
459	            self._execute_video(other)
460	        elif isinstance(other, SglVariable):
461	            self._execute_variable(other)
462	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
463	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
464	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
465	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
466	        elif isinstance(other, SglCommitLazy):
467	            self._execute_commit_lazy_operations(other)
468	        elif isinstance(other, SglConcateAndAppend):
469	            if (
470	                global_config.enable_parallel_encoding
471	                and self.backend.support_concate_and_append
472	            ):
473	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(other)
474	            else:
475	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_text(other)
476	        elif isinstance(other, SglSeparateReasoning):
477	            self._execute_separate_reasoning(other)
478	        else:
479	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type: {type(other)}")
480	
481	    def _execute_fill(self, value: str, prefix=False):
482	        value = str(value)
483	
484	        if (
485	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
486	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
487	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
488	            and not prefix
489	        ):
490	            self.backend.spec_fill(value)
491	            return
492	
493	        if self.speculated_text.startswith(value):
494	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[len(value) :]
495	        else:
496	            self.speculated_text = ""
497	
498	        self.text_ += value
499	
500	    def _execute_image(self, expr: SglImage):
501	        path = expr.path
502	
503	        base64_data = encode_image_base64(path)
504	
505	        self.images_.append((path, base64_data))
506	        self.cur_images.append((path, base64_data))
507	        self.text_ += self.chat_template.image_token
508	
509	    def _execute_video(self, expr: SglVideo):
510	        path = expr.path
511	        num_frames = expr.num_frames
512	
513	        base64_data = encode_video_base64(path, num_frames)
514	
515	        self.images_.append((path, base64_data))
516	        self.cur_images.append((path, base64_data))
517	        self.text_ += self.chat_template.image_token
518	
519	    def _spec_gen(self, sampling_params):
520	        stop = sampling_params.stop
521	        max_new_tokens = sampling_params.max_new_tokens
522	        meta_info = {}
523	
524	        def regen():
525	            nonlocal meta_info
526	
527	            sampling_params.max_new_tokens = max(
528	                sampling_params.max_new_tokens, self.num_api_spec_tokens
529	            )
530	            sampling_params.stop = None
531	            self.speculated_text, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
532	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
533	            )
534	
535	        def find_stop():
536	            if isinstance(stop, str):
537	                return self.speculated_text.find(stop)
538	            elif isinstance(stop, (tuple, list)):
539	                pos = -1
540	                for stop_str in stop:
541	                    stop_pos = self.speculated_text.find(stop_str)
542	                    if stop_pos != -1 and (pos == -1 or stop_pos < pos):
543	                        pos = stop_pos
544	                return pos
545	            else:
546	                raise Exception("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
547	
548	        if stop is None:
549	            if len(self.speculated_text) < max_new_tokens:
550	                regen()
551	            comp = self.speculated_text[:max_new_tokens]
552	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[max_new_tokens:]
553	        elif isinstance(stop, (str, list, tuple)):
554	            if self.speculated_text == "":
555	                regen()
556	            stop_pos = find_stop()
557	            if stop_pos == -1:
558	                stop_pos = min(
559	                    sampling_params.max_new_tokens,
560	                    len(self.speculated_text),
561	                )
562	            comp = self.speculated_text[:stop_pos]
563	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[stop_pos:]
564	        else:
565	            raise ValueError("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
566	
567	        return comp, meta_info
568	
569	    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
570	        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
571	        name = expr.name
572	        if not self.stream:
573	            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
574	                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
575	                    self,
576	                    sampling_params=sampling_params,
577	                )
578	
579	            else:
580	                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
581	                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
582	                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
583	                    # They will be lazily executed later.
584	                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
585	                        self,
586	                        sampling_params=sampling_params,
587	                        spec_var_name=name,
588	                    )
589	                    return
590	
591	                else:  # Speculative execution on models with completion interface
592	                    comp, meta_info = self._spec_gen(sampling_params)
593	            if isinstance(comp, list):
594	                self.text_ += comp[0]
595	            else:
596	                assert isinstance(comp, str)
597	                self.text_ += comp
598	
599	            self.variables[name] = comp

2534	
2535	        Args:
2536	            logits_output: The logits output from the model forward
2537	            forward_batch: The forward batch that generates logits_output
2538	
2539	        Returns:
2540	            A list of next_token_ids
2541	        """
2542	        # For duplex models with multiple output streams.
2543	        if isinstance(logits_output, tuple):
2544	            return torch.stack(
2545	                [self.sample(values, forward_batch) for values in logits_output],
2546	                axis=-1,
2547	            )
2548	
2549	        self._preprocess_logits(logits_output, forward_batch.sampling_info)
2550	        # Sample the next tokens
2551	        next_token_ids = self.sampler(
2552	            logits_output,
2553	            forward_batch.sampling_info,
2554	            forward_batch.return_logprob,
2555	            forward_batch.top_logprobs_nums,
2556	            forward_batch.token_ids_logprobs,
2557	            # For prefill, we only use the position of the last token.
2558	            (
2559	                forward_batch.positions
2560	                if forward_batch.forward_mode.is_decode()
2561	                else forward_batch.seq_lens - 1
2562	            ),
2563	        )
2564	        return next_token_ids
2565	
2566	    def compute_logprobs_only(
2567	        self,
2568	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,
2569	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
2570	    ) -> None:
2571	        """
2572	        Compute token_ids_logprobs without performing sampling.
2573	
2574	        Optimized path for prefill-only requests that need token_ids_logprobs but don't
2575	        require next token generation. Skips expensive sampling operations
2576	        while still providing requested probability information.
2577	
2578	        Args:
2579	            logits_output: The logits output from the model forward
2580	            forward_batch: The forward batch that generates logits_output
2581	        """
2582	        if not forward_batch.token_ids_logprobs:
2583	            return
2584	
2585	        # Preprocess logits (same as in sample method)
2586	        self._preprocess_logits(logits_output, forward_batch.sampling_info)
2587	
2588	        # Delegate to sampler for logprob-only computation
2589	        # This populates logits_output with requested token probabilities
2590	        self.sampler.compute_logprobs_only(
2591	            logits_output,
2592	            forward_batch.sampling_info,
2593	            forward_batch.return_logprob,
2594	            forward_batch.top_logprobs_nums,
2595	            forward_batch.token_ids_logprobs,
2596	        )
2597	
2598	    @property
2599	    def model_is_mrope(self) -> bool:
2600	        """Detect if the model has "mrope" rope_scaling type.
2601	        mrope requires keep "rope_deltas" between prompt and decoding phases."""
2602	        rope_scaling = getattr(
2603	            self.model_config.hf_text_config, "rope_parameters", None
2604	        ) or getattr(self.model_config.hf_text_config, "rope_scaling", {})
2605	        if rope_scaling is None:
2606	            return False
2607	        is_mrope_enabled = "mrope_section" in rope_scaling
2608	        return is_mrope_enabled
2609

17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

570	        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
571	        name = expr.name
572	        if not self.stream:
573	            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
574	                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
575	                    self,
576	                    sampling_params=sampling_params,

578	
579	            else:
580	                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
581	                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
582	                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
583	                    # They will be lazily executed later.
584	                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
585	                        self,

587	                        spec_var_name=name,
588	                    )
589	                    return

593	            if isinstance(comp, list):

606	            generator = self.backend.generate_stream(

609	
610	            self.variables[name] = ""

612	
613	            for comp, meta_info in generator:

617	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()

714	    def _execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(self, expr: SglConcateAndAppend):
715	        self_len = len(self.text_)
716	
717	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
718	            exe = s.stream_executor
719	            exe.submit(SglCommitLazy())
720	
721	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
722	            exe = s.stream_executor

725	            self.text_ += exe.text_[exe.fork_start_text_pos :]

730	    def _execute_separate_reasoning(self, expr: SglSeparateReasoning):
731	        if self.stream:
732	            # separate reasoning for stream is not supported
733	            return
734	
735	        if (
736	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
737	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
738	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
739	        ):
740	            # Execute the stored lazy generation calls
741	            self.backend.role_end_generate(self)
742	
743	        from sglang.srt.parser.reasoning_parser import ReasoningParser
744	
745	        reasoning_parser = ReasoningParser(expr.model_type)
746	        other = expr.expr
747	        if not other:
748	            return
749	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen) or isinstance(other, SglSelect):
750	            cur_text = self.get_var(other.name)
751	            reasoning, normal_text = reasoning_parser.parse_non_stream(cur_text)
752	            reasoning_name = expr.process_name_for_reasoning(other.name)
753	            self.set_var(other.name, normal_text)
754	            self.set_var(reasoning_name, reasoning)
755	            # the variable is ready to be used
756	            self.variable_event[reasoning_name].set()
757	            self.text_ = self.text_[: self.cur_role_begin_pos] + normal_text
758	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
759	            for x in other.expr_list:
760	                self._execute_separate_reasoning(
761	                    SglSeparateReasoning(expr.model_type, x)
762	                )

766	            expr, (SglGen, SglSelect, SglVarScopeBegin, SglSeparateReasoning)
767	        ):
768	            self.variable_event[expr.name] = threading.Event()
769	            if self.stream:
770	                self.stream_var_event[expr.name] = threading.Event()
771	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
772	            for e in expr.expr_list:
773	                self._init_var_event(e)
